name: Evolv Technologies Holdings, Inc.

profile.description: Evolv Technologies develops, manufactures, and sells AI-powered security screening systems for high-traffic venues. Its flagship Evolv Express uses advanced sensors and machine learning to detect weapons in unstructured people flows without requiring individuals to stop, remove items, or empty pockets. The company serves 1,200+ customers across education, healthcare, sports, entertainment, and government sectors and has scanned over 4 billion people since 2019.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Evolv grew revenue 40% YoY to $145.9M in 2025 and turned Adjusted EBITDA positive ($11.1M) for the first time, demonstrating successful commercialization of its AI-powered screening platform [1][2]. The company launched Evolv eXpedite for bag screening, expanding from people-only to comprehensive venue security. AI Deployment (2/2): Evolv Express IS an AI product — its core value proposition is real-time AI weapon detection that has screened 4+ billion people, replacing legacy metal detectors with touchless, AI-driven threat identification across 1,200+ customer sites [2]. Market Share (1/2): Evolv is the clear category leader in AI-powered touchless weapon detection, deployed at major venues including NFL stadiums, schools, and hospitals, but faces increasing competition from traditional security vendors adding AI capabilities [2]. Pipeline (1/2): 2026 guidance of $172-$178M revenue (18-22% growth) with ARR reaching $145-$150M; shift to ~50% pure subscription model for new deployments strengthens recurring revenue visibility [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Continuous investment in AI algorithms, sensor technology, and analytics platform with the Evolv Insights product expanding the data offering [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Evolv's mission to 'make the world a safer place' through AI-powered security is clear and connects to public safety, but is not deeply distinctive — many security companies express similar goals. The 'privacy by design' approach differentiates somewhat [4]. Community Impact (1/2): Evolv serves schools, houses of worship, and healthcare facilities, directly protecting vulnerable populations; the company has screened 4+ billion people, which represents meaningful social impact in an era of mass shootings [2]. Employee Retention (0/2): Glassdoor rating of 3.1/5 based on 46 reviews indicates mixed employee sentiment; reports of overwork/burnout culture raised at all-hands meetings, and the CEO termination in October 2024 'to improve company culture' raises concerns [3]. Insider Buying (1/2): New CEO John Kedzierski appointed December 2024 has equity exposure; however, the forced departure of the prior CEO Peter George without cause creates leadership instability rather than insider conviction [3].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Evolv's financial profile reflects a high-growth but still unprofitable company transitioning toward sustainable economics. Revenue grew 40% YoY to $145.9M in 2025 with 2026 guidance of $172-$178M (18-22% growth) [2]. D/E of 35.4% is healthy with $69M cash against $42.2M debt [1]. However, ROE of -28.0% and profit margin of -22.7% reflect continued operating losses despite turning Adjusted EBITDA positive ($11.1M in 2025) [1][2]. Forward P/E of -228.7 is meaningless for a loss-making company and scores at floor. The 3yr revenue CAGR of ~40% (from $52.7M in 2022 to $145.9M in 2025) is exceptional and scores at target [1]. Liquidity floor easily cleared at $18.6M daily dollar volume (3.05M avg vol × $6.10) [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Evolv IS an AI company — its entire product line is built on proprietary AI/ML weapon detection algorithms trained on millions of scans across 1,200+ customer sites [2]. AI does not threaten this business; AI IS the business. The physical hardware (sensors, screening gates) combined with proprietary training data from 4+ billion scans creates a moat that pure-software AI cannot replicate. The shift toward subscription/SaaS model with Evolv Insights analytics further embeds AI into recurring revenue. Venues cannot deploy a ChatGPT alternative to screen for weapons — this requires purpose-built hardware-software integration. AI Opportunity (1/1): 100% of Evolv's $145.9M revenue is AI-derived, with Evolv Express and eXpedite being AI-native products and Evolv Insights providing AI-powered analytics as an upsell [2].

infinite_game.summary: Evolv shows elements of infinite game thinking through its mission-driven focus on public safety and long-term subscription model transition, but the forced CEO departure in 2024 and cultural concerns undermine the organizational foundation needed for sustained infinite play.

just_cause.analysis: Protecting people from weapons in schools, hospitals, and public venues connects to a genuine just cause — making public spaces safer in an era of mass violence [2]. However, the mission statement 'make the world a safer place' is relatively generic, and the company's execution of this cause has been inconsistent given leadership turmoil and cultural issues flagged by the board itself [3].

trusting_teams.analysis: The board terminated CEO Peter George in October 2024 specifically citing the need 'to improve the company's culture,' which is a direct indictment of the trust environment [3]. Glassdoor rating of 3.1/5 with reports of overwork and burnout culture raised at all-hands meetings suggests low psychological safety. Three CEO changes in rapid succession (George → Ellenbogen interim → Kedzierski) creates organizational instability [3].

worthy_rivals.analysis: No evidence found of Evolv management acknowledging worthy rivals in a constructive, learning-oriented way. The company positions itself as the category creator and leader in AI-powered screening, but traditional security players like Smiths Detection and Garrett are adding AI capabilities that Evolv should acknowledge as competitive motivation [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: The shift from hardware sales to a subscription/SaaS model (targeting 50% pure subscription for new deployments in 2026) represents meaningful business model evolution [2]. The expansion from people screening (Express) to bag screening (eXpedite) and analytics (Insights) shows the company is building platform capabilities rather than remaining a single-product vendor.

courage_to_lead.analysis: The board's decision to terminate the CEO for cultural reasons rather than tolerating dysfunction shows some courage to prioritize organizational health over continuity [3]. The subscription model transition sacrifices near-term revenue recognition for long-term recurring revenue quality. However, the company has not yet demonstrated courage through investing during adversity or walking away from misaligned revenue.

investment_thesis: Evolv Technologies is the category leader in AI-powered touchless weapon detection, serving 1,200+ customers including NFL stadiums, schools, and hospitals with technology that has screened over 4 billion people [2]. The company grew revenue 40% YoY to $145.9M in 2025, turned Adjusted EBITDA positive for the first time at $11.1M, and guided 2026 revenue to $172-$178M (18-22% growth) with ARR reaching $145-$150M [2]. The transition to ~50% pure subscription model strengthens recurring revenue quality. With $69M cash and improving unit economics, Evolv is approaching a profitability inflection point in a growing market driven by school safety mandates and venue security regulations.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on profitability uncertainty and leadership instability. Despite 40% revenue growth, Evolv posted a -$33.1M net loss in 2025 and ROE of -28% — the path to GAAP profitability remains unclear beyond Adjusted EBITDA [1][2]. The forced CEO termination in October 2024 for cultural reasons, followed by two leadership changes in three months, creates organizational risk and execution uncertainty under new CEO John Kedzierski [3]. The $1.1B market cap implies ~6x forward revenue, which demands sustained 20%+ growth that could be challenged by traditional security companies (Smiths Detection, Garrett) adding AI capabilities to their established customer relationships. Evolv's technology has faced scrutiny over detection accuracy, and any high-profile failure at a protected venue would be catastrophic for the brand. Stock-based compensation remains elevated, diluting shareholder value.
key_catalysts: 2026 revenue guidance of $172-$178M with ARR of $145-$150M represents 20%+ growth and continued subscription model transition driving recurring revenue quality [2]
School safety mandate legislation in multiple U.S. states could accelerate education sector adoption, Evolv's fastest-growing vertical
Full-year GAAP profitability inflection as subscription revenue scales and operating leverage improves from the $11.1M Adjusted EBITDA base [2]
key_risks: Leadership instability — three CEO changes in 14 months (George terminated, Ellenbogen interim, Kedzierski appointed) creates execution risk during a critical growth phase [3]
Detection accuracy scrutiny — any high-profile weapon incident at an Evolv-protected venue would severely damage brand credibility and could trigger customer defections
GAAP profitability remains elusive despite Adjusted EBITDA turning positive, with -$33.1M net loss in 2025 and elevated stock-based compensation diluting shareholder value [2]
